title: bf16 hybrid GPU weight storage (Plan B for PoC)
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

At f32, Llama 3 8B is ~32 GB and won't fit in 24 GB VRAM. At bf16, 16 GB fits. For the PoC we use a hybrid path: weights stored as bf16 in GPU buffers, activations stay f32. Uses existing gpu_matmul_bf16 (ferrotorch-gpu/src/blas.rs:909) which does f32->bf16 matmul->f32 on-device.

Work:
- Add GpuBufferHandle variant or sidecar metadata for Dtype::BF16 buffers.
- StateDict -> GPU loader: for each tensor, on-device convert f32->bf16 via existing gpu_f32_to_bf16 kernel (ferrotorch-gpu/src/kernels.rs:719, launcher at line 16775), then free the f32 buffer. Net VRAM cost = 2 bytes/param instead of 4.
- Teach Linear::forward (and GQA q_proj/k_proj/v_proj/o_proj) to dispatch to gpu_matmul_bf16 when weight buffer dtype is bf16.
- Embedding lookup: bf16 rows, upcast to f32 on gather (cheap; 1 row per token).
- RMSNorm weight: bf16, upcast in the kernel (already does fp arithmetic).

Explicitly OUT of scope (tracked separately):
- Native bf16 Float trait impl in ferrotorch-core (Plan A; follow-up issue).
- bf16 on CPU, bf16 for gradients/optimizer state, mixed-precision training.

Acceptance:
- Llama-3-8B loaded end-to-end using <18 GB peak VRAM (measured via nvidia-smi during forward pass).
- Generated token IDs match a reference implementation (vLLM / transformers) for a fixed seed/prompt within an acceptable top-k overlap (>95% top-5 token agreement for a 32-token continuation).
